title: Portfolio Website
description: A modern, data-driven React portfolio with polished navigation, interactive backgrounds, reusable components, and recruiter-focused content architecture.
details: Built with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and ReactBits-inspired visual components.
Implemented interactive Galaxy background, collapsible navigation, responsive pages, and JSON-driven content.
Designed dedicated pages for About, Contact & CV, Extracurricular Activities, and future project/internship expansion.

title: Foolics API
description: A Foodics internship backend API for an online ordering system, built to support a separate frontend team with documented auth, cart, order, stock, and admin workflows.
details: Developed Express REST APIs for user registration/login, product lookup, cart management, order creation, admin dashboard data, stock updates, and order-status transitions.
Integrated PostgreSQL with Prisma models for users, carts, cart items, products, orders, and order items, with JWT authentication and admin authorization middleware.
Documented the API with Swagger UI, added Jest and Supertest coverage for the core endpoints, and provided Docker setup for local handoff to the frontend team.

media.alt: Foolics API architecture and endpoint overview

mediaNote: Custom API overview image used because the frontend that consumed this backend is not currently available.

title: Foolix Foodics Frontend Internship App
description: A deployed Vue 3 ordering app for Foolix with role-based cashier POS and operations dashboard experiences built during the Foodics frontend internship.
details: Built one Vite and Vue 3 codebase for cashier and admin workflows, with login-gated role-based routing.
Implemented a shared API client, Pinia state management, menu browsing, cart and checkout flows, dashboard orders board, and menu management screens.
Deployed on Vercel at https://foodics-internship-2026-fe.vercel.app with history-mode route rewrites for direct links and refreshes.

media.alt: Foolix Foodics frontend admin dashboard walkthrough

mediaNote: Captured from the deployed Foolix admin dashboard on Vercel.

title: Travel Web Application
description: A full-stack travel website where users can browse destinations, create accounts, search places, and manage a want-to-go list.
details: Built a client-server full-stack web application using Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, EJS, JavaScript, and HTML.
Implemented authentication, sessions, secure routing, and access control.
Designed MongoDB CRUD operations, duplicate validation, destination pages, want-to-go lists, and substring-based search.
mediaNote: Captured locally; authenticated flow requires a MongoDB connection string.

title: HR Management System
description: A full-stack HR management web application for payroll, attendance, leave approvals, and role-based administration.
details: Developed with ASP.NET and SQL Server using Admin, HR, and Employee access roles.
Designed relational database procedures, functions, and views for payroll, attendance, deductions, leave workflows, and approvals.
Implemented authentication, automated payroll calculations, performance tracking, and frontend-backend validation.

media.alt: HR Management System admin dashboard preview

mediaNote: Captured from the DB3 ASP.NET Web Forms dashboard styling with representative HR data; full runtime requires IIS/Visual Studio Web Application targets and SQL Server LocalDB.

title: Operating System Simulator
description: A C-based simulation of monolithic operating system concepts with process scheduling, memory management, disk swapping, and resource mutexes.
details: Built a custom text-based program interpreter for OS course workflows.
Implemented memory management with disk swapping and synchronized resource access.
Compared multiple process scheduling algorithms and simulated core OS behavior.
mediaNote: Captured from the local Kernel Studio dashboard.

title: Computer Architecture Processor
description: A C-based simulation of a pipelined Harvard Architecture processor for the Computer Systems Architecture course at GUC.
details: Implemented a custom 16-bit instruction set architecture.
Built a 3-stage instruction pipeline with hazard handling.
Simulated processor behavior for the Double McHarvard package specification.
mediaNote: Captured from the local static processor GUI.

title: Special Folio
description: A React portfolio platform for students, instructors, employers, and admins to showcase projects, manage portfolios, and connect around internships.
details: Built a multi-role front-end prototype with authentication, protected routes, dashboards, portfolio browsing, course workflows, messaging, and notifications.
Implemented student, instructor, employer, and admin experiences using React Router, local state, dummy data, charts, and reusable layout components.
Captured a local walkthrough covering login, project browsing, and statistics screens.
mediaNote: Captured from the local React/Vite app.
